query: MentalHappy 3.0 complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Hosting Support Groups - MentalHappy vs. Facebook
results.content:
#### >>Amanda Lee – Women’s Empowerment Facilitator

"Switching to MentalHappy changed everything for me and my group. I was running empowerment circles on Facebook, and that was really exhausting. I felt like the group was losing its purpose between spam posts and endless distractions. From the time I've moved to MentalHappy, I’ve seen a huge boost in engagement. The platform feels peaceful, focused, and actually designed for meaningful conversations. I’ve even monetized my premium groups without needing to piece together different tools. Now, my participants show up regularly and feel supported every step of the way. It’s not just easier for me—it’s better for them too."

#### >>Dan Peters – Grief Recovery Coach [...] Lack of Structure – Facebook is designed for casual interaction, not structured support group facilitation. There are no tools for tracking participant progress, scheduling sessions, or handling payments.
 Distractions – Facebook’s platform is full of ads and unrelated notifications, which can distract members from the core purpose of the group.
 Privacy Concerns – Facebook is not HIPAA-compliant or built for sensitive mental health discussions. Even in private groups, personal data is vulnerable to breaches.
 Limited Professional Tools – Facilitators must manage everything manually. There are no built-in features for automated scheduling, payment processing, or outcome tracking. [...] Facebook Groups are free… but not really. Sure, you don’t pay upfront, but the hours spent managing everything manually add up. And if you want to charge for your group, good luck figuring that out through multiple platforms. Sometimes, free time and frustration cost more.

### Data and Insights

MentalHappy shows you how your group is doing—who’s attending, who’s dropping off, and how people are progressing. It’s like having a report card for your group’s success. You can easily make adjustments to help your participants thrive.

On Facebook? You’ll know who liked a post… and that’s about it. There’s no way to see real progress or track whether the group is making a difference. You’re left guessing, hoping people are benefiting.

### MentalHappy – Your Mental Health Partner

results.title: Expert-Led Support Groups, Online | MentalHappy
results.content:
To be clear about what this is and isn't: MentalHappy groups are psychoeducational and peer support — a supplemental form of care that helps people learn, cope, and connect. They are not therapy or medical treatment, and they're designed to complement professional care, not replace it. MentalHappy does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

That honesty is the point. We're the part of care that's been missing for too many people: accessible, human, ongoing support between everything else.

Who we're for

## Whatever brought you here, there's a place for you.
